brand_description: High-drama, high-control alt-rap built around elastic flows, theatrical vocals, sharp technical precision, Southern-rooted swagger, swampy atmosphere, genre-fluid production, and fearless character work. The sound sits primarily in hip-hop but pulls from boom-bap, alt-R&B, pop-rap, house, punk energy, dance music, spoken word, and experimental internet-era pop.

audience.description: Fans of technically sharp, theatrical, genre-bending rap with bold personality, emotional volatility, inventive vocal delivery, crisp drums, strange textures, and memorable scene-like performances.
audience.guardrail: Can be intense, provocative, strange, funny, confrontational, and emotionally raw, but avoid slurs, direct threats, copied artist signatures, exploitative shock value, or lazy caricature. Make clean edits possible.

character.name: CHOCHEE
character.core_concept: A theatrical alt-rap shapeshifter: technically serious, emotionally messy, funny, stylish, swampy, aggressive, vulnerable, and impossible to box in.
character.fallback_summary: Genre-fluid alt-rap with elastic flows, actorly vocals, crisp drums, rubbery bass, off-kilter synths, swampy Southern textures, sudden beat pivots, sharp hooks, and fearless character shifts.

positioning.one_liner: Theatrical alt-rap with elastic flows, swampy Southern attitude, genre-fluid beats, actorly vocals, technical bite, and chaotic charisma.

voice.persona: fearless, shape-shifting, witty, confrontational, emotionally exposed, stylishly unhinged, and fully in control
voice.tone: dramatic, funny, sharp, volatile, self-aware, technically precise, and unpredictable
avoid: direct imitation of any specific artist

music.default_prompt: Create an original theatrical alt-rap track for CHOCHEE. The track should feel like high-drama, high-control rap performance: technically sharp hip-hop built around elastic flows, sudden cadence changes, actorly vocal choices, and bold character shifts. Keep hip-hop at the center, but blend Southern rap confidence, boom-bap bite, alt-R&B melody, pop-rap immediacy, dance-floor pulse, punk attitude, spoken-word tension, and experimental internet-era pop weirdness. The vocal performance should feel like a one-person stage show inside a rap record: barked bars, whispered asides, deadpan comedy, melodic hooks, breathy pauses, chants, shrieks, and conversational confession used as musical tools. Production should feature crisp drums, rubbery bass, humid low-end pressure, off-kilter synths, negative space, strange percussion, and sudden structural pivots. Every track needs at least one recognizable sonic signature: a swampy synth riff, distorted vocal chop, rubber bass phrase, creepy piano figure, sharp chant, or percussive motif that makes the song identifiable. Lyrics should mix ego, insecurity, ambition, sexuality, anxiety, self-mythology, dark humor, social observation, and emotional exposure. A song can feel like therapy, battle rap, sketch comedy, diary entry, and villain monologue all at once. Keep the result original, scene-like, stylish, strange, technically athletic, and hard to box in. Avoid copying any specific artist's flow, lyrics, vocal tone, cadence, slogans, melody, or production signature.
music.default_style: theatrical alt-rap, genre-fluid hip-hop, elastic flows, actorly vocals, Southern rap swagger, swampy atmosphere, crisp drums, rubbery bass, off-kilter synths, alt-R&B hooks, punk energy, dance pulse, spoken-word tension, experimental pop weirdness
sonic_palette: crisp forward drums with clean transient punch
rubbery bassline with humid low-end pressure
vocal ad-libs that feel like character voices
production_do: Make the vocal performance the lead instrument: dynamic, character-driven, and rhythmically athletic.
Use sudden flow, beat, and tone changes while keeping the track focused and intentional.
Keep drums crisp, bass rubbery, and the vocal clear.
Build a recognizable non-vocal sonic motif that returns throughout the song.
Use negative space, beat mutes, and abrupt drops to heighten drama.
Let hooks be memorable without becoming generic or overly glossy.
Blend strange textures with clean modern mix quality.
Make each song feel like a scene with a beginning, escalation, twist, and payoff.
production_avoid: generic trap loops with no identity
overcrowded arrangements that bury the vocal

beat_signature.priority: The beat should feel like a theatrical set: minimal when tension matters, explosive when the character snaps, and strange enough to be instantly recognizable.
beat_signature.drum_feel: Crisp, modern, agile drums that can swing between boom-bap bite, Southern bounce, dance pulse, and punky impact.
beat_signature.low_end: Rubbery, humid, physical bass that moves around the vocal without muddying it.
beat_signature.instrumental_hook: Every song needs a repeatable signature motif: swampy synth riff, distorted vocal chop, rubber bass phrase, creepy piano figure, chant phrase, or strange percussion loop.

lyrics.required_closing: End with a decisive final hook, character flip, chant reprise, sudden punchline, spoken-word sting, or instrumental-motif outro. Avoid unresolved fade-outs unless the final motif clearly lands as the last scene.
lyrics.hook_style: short, strange, chantable phrases with sharp rhythmic bite, supported by a recognizable instrumental motif or vocal character switch

visuals.album_art_direction: swamp-glam theatrical portrait language without direct facial likeness; humid neon, mud-gloss textures, sharp teeth motifs, backstage shadows, wet concrete, insect-like highlights, distorted mirrors, bold typography, high contrast, strange elegance, and controlled chaos

guardrails.originality: Use broad genre and mood signals only. Do not copy melodies, lyrics, arrangements, vocal signatures, cover art, names, slogans, flows, ad-libs, or distinctive production choices from existing artists.
